name: node-env-paths
title: node-env-paths
summary: Node.js support for XDG Directory Path
description:
Node.js support for XDG Directory Path
This package allows one to create nodejs application specific path
in XDG directory, optionaly creating the needed sub-directory.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-env-paths because Node.js support for XDG Directory Path.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-env-paths as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-env-paths with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-env-paths --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
